description: Single config DocType for the Wave <> Hypa integration. All rules live here so admins can edit them without a deploy.

fields.description: Master kill-switch. When off, the webhook endpoint returns 503 and no outbound calls are made.
fields.label: Integration Enabled

fields.description: When on, customer resolution falls back to matching by email after the primary wave_customer_id lookup misses. Lets the integration adopt existing ERP customers (imported, manually entered, or re-registered under a new Wave _id) instead of creating duplicates. Strict: declines on ambiguous matches (multiple ERP customers share the email, or the single match is already linked to a different Wave id) and logs to the Frappe Error Log. Default off so existing sites keep the wave_id-only behaviour until ops opts in.
fields.label: Customer Email Fallback Lookup Enabled

fields.description: When on, ORDER.UPDATE webhooks with pickerStatus=COLLECTED reconcile the ERP Pick List (set picked_qty + batch_no from Wave's payload) and auto-submit any Pick List in Draft state. Submitted/cancelled Pick Lists are annotated with Wave's reported pick state via Frappe Comments only; their docstatus is never touched. Submit happens through the existing Pick List Wave Override permission gate via the inbound flag. Default off so existing sites are no-ops until ops opts in.
fields.label: Pick List Inbound Submit Enabled

fields.description: Item Tax Template applied per-line to the SHIPPING_COST fee line at intake. When set, the intake handler back-calculates the line rate from Wave's shipping amount and this template's effective tax rate so the line total always equals Wave's amount (rate + tax = Wave's amount). Leave blank to use today's behaviour (rate = Wave's amount, line subject to the SO's master Sales Taxes and Charges Template).
fields.label: Shipping Item Tax Template

fields.description: When on, an SO drafted with wave_manual_review_required=1 (unresolved items, missing fee mappings, missing tax templates) also creates a Frappe ToDo so the team is pinged in 'My Tasks' without needing Wave Sync Log access. Recipient resolved from Assignee first, then Role. Default off.
fields.label: Intake Review ToDo Enabled

fields.description: Single User to receive intake-review ToDos. Wins over the Role field below when set. Leave blank to fan out to every user in the configured Role instead.
fields.label: Intake Review Assignee

fields.description: Role whose members receive intake-review ToDos when no single Assignee is set. One ToDo per user with this role.
fields.label: Intake Review Role

fields.description: Divisor applied to every Wave amount to convert to major currency units. Wave sends amounts in minor units (cents); leave as 100 unless that changes.
fields.label: Price Scale Divisor

fields.description: Wave Sync Log rows older than this many days are deleted by the daily retention task.
fields.label: Log Retention (days)

fields.description: Shared secret. Wave must send this value in the x-api-key HTTP header on every webhook. Must be exactly 32 URL-safe characters (A-Z, a-z, 0-9, underscore, or hyphen only) — generate with `python -c 'import secrets; print(secrets.token_urlsafe(24))'`. Non-URL-safe characters (e.g. £, $, &, ', <, >, /, %) are rejected because they break in HTTP headers, shells, or proxies. Stored encrypted.
fields.label: Inbound API Key (32 URL-safe chars)

fields.description: Base URL for the Wave REST API, e.g. https://dev.hypaafrica.api.wavegrocery.com
fields.label: Wave API Base URL

fields.description: Sent as the appId HTTP header on every outbound call. Provided by Wave when the app is registered.
fields.label: Wave App ID

fields.description: Identifier of the Wave store this ERP instance mirrors. Single-shop deployments leave this as 1; sent in the JSON body of stock-sync calls.
fields.label: Wave Store ID

fields.description: API key sent on every outbound call to Wave (X-API-Key header). Stored encrypted.
fields.label: Wave API Key

fields.description: Master kill-switch for outbound stock pushes. When off, ERP stock movements are still logged but no HTTP call is made to Wave. Use this to mute traffic during cutovers without disabling the inbound integration.
fields.label: Outbound Stock Sync Enabled

fields.description: Master kill-switch for outbound order-status pushes. When off, Sales Order submit/cancel events are observed and logged but no PUT is made to Wave. Independent from the stock kill-switch so ops can mute one channel at a time.
fields.label: Outbound Order Status Sync Enabled

fields.description: When on, every Pick List creation triggers a push of the picked items + batch numbers to Wave. Independent of the Pick List ACCEPTED status push (which is gated by the Outbound Status Rules table — add/remove the (Pick List, after_insert) row to control that channel). Off by default; flip on once Wave's batch-IDs endpoint payload is wired in services/wave_client.
fields.label: Pick List Batch IDs Push Enabled

fields.description: What identifier the picker app should scan for each Pick List line. Blank (default) sends batch numbers from ERPNext's FEFO/FIFO allocation — one identifier per batch row. 'Item Code' sends the SKU itself, consolidated to one identifier per SKU. 'Item Barcode' sends the first row of the Item's Barcodes child table, also one per SKU; throws when the Item has no barcode rows. The outbound payload shape (productId + batchIds) is unchanged across modes; the inbound ORDER.UPDATE handler validates Wave's reported identifier against whatever mode is active.
fields.label: Picker Identifier Source

fields.description: When on, ERP-side Pick List submit and cancel are restricted to users with the 'Pick List Wave Override' role (System Manager always passes). Use this once Wave is the source of truth for picking — direct ERP submissions then become a manager-only escape hatch. Default off so existing sites keep working until ops decides to enforce.
fields.label: Pick List ERP Submit Lockdown Enabled

fields.description: Company applied to every Sales Order created by this integration.
fields.label: Default Company

fields.description: Warehouse used as set_warehouse on every Sales Order.
fields.label: Default Warehouse

fields.description: Selling Price List that drives line-item rates. ERP is the pricing source of truth; Wave's prices are ignored.
fields.label: Default Price List

fields.description: Currency for every Sales Order created by this integration.
fields.label: Default Currency

fields.description: Placeholder Item used as the single fallback line when an ORDER.CREATE webhook arrives but ZERO of its Wave SKUs resolve to ERP Items. Without this set, such orders are aborted with a loud Error + ToDo. Recommended setup: create an Item named e.g. 'Wave Unresolved SKU Placeholder' with a parking income account; the SO it lands on will be flagged for manual review and re-keyed by the operator.
fields.label: Default Unresolved Items Placeholder

fields.description: Customer Group assigned to Customers newly created from Wave webhooks.
fields.label: Default Customer Group

fields.description: Territory assigned to Customers newly created from Wave webhooks.
fields.label: Default Territory

fields.description: Fallback Customer for guest orders (payload isGuest=true). Must exist in ERP before orders are processed.
fields.label: Walk-in Customer

fields.description: All rule tables are editable here. The integration reads them at dispatch time, so changes take effect immediately after save.
fields.label: Rules

fields.description: Routes (Wave doc_type, action) pairs to a registered handler. Disabled rows are skipped.
fields.label: Route Rules

fields.description: Inbound status transitions. Each row tells the order update handler what to do when Wave reports the given status.
fields.label: Inbound Status Rules

fields.description: Outbound status pushes. Each row maps an ERP lifecycle event to the status string sent to Wave's changeorderstatus endpoint.
fields.label: Outbound Status Rules

fields.description: Wave fees (SHIPPING_COST, PLASTIC_BAGS, ...) are added as Sales Order lines using the ERP Item configured here.
fields.label: Fee Mappings

fields.description: Sales Taxes and Charges Templates stamped on every draft Sales Order created from ORDER.CREATE webhooks. The first enabled row whose template exists and is not disabled wins; ERPNext copies the template's tax rows into the Sales Order automatically.
fields.label: Tax Rules

fields.description: Maps Wave's paymentType strings to a classification (prepaid|cod) and an ERP Mode of Payment. Read at order intake to stamp wave_payment_classification on the Sales Order, and at Payment Entry submit to validate that the right MOP is being used for the order's payment class.
fields.label: Payment Method Mappings

name: Wave Settings
